message:
chore: sync beads + minor follow-ups

- Beads: close bd-lff4p.1.8, bd-lff4p.5.8; close bd-lff4p.4.3; update status for ongoing lff4p work
- ftui-tty: record initial winsize + keep SIGWINCH guard/channel alive in event source
- frankenterm-core: remove explicit panic! from parser tests
- e2e validator: remove unused imports/vars

files.patch:
@@ -1326,7 +1326,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.1.5","title":"Implement: selection model + copy extraction","description":"Goal
- Implement selection primitives (char/cell/word/line) and copy extraction from grid+scrollback.

Requirements
- Coordinate model for selections spanning scrollback and viewport.
- Word selection heuristics tuned for code and paths.

Acceptance Criteria
- Deterministic tests for selection across wrapped lines and resize.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:49.789352547Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:49.789352547Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.5","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:49.789352547Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.5","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:49.789352547Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.1.6","title":"Implement: VT/ANSI parser state machine (CSI/OSC/SGR + edits)","description":"Goal
- Implement the output stream parser and apply mutations to the grid.

Must Support (initial)
- CSI cursor moves, erase in line/screen, insert/delete char/line, scroll up/down, set scroll region.
- SGR attributes and colors (16/256/truecolor).
- OSC 0/2 (title) (optional storage) and OSC 8 hyperlinks.

Acceptance Criteria
- Conformance fixtures from bd-lff4p.1.1 replay to exact expected grid snapshots.
- Proptest: parser never panics on arbitrary byte streams.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:56.955554647Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T09:18:10.202126850Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.6","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:56.955554647Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.6","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:56.955554647Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.6","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:56.955554647Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.6","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:18:10.202088217Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.6","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:56.955554647Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.1.7","title":"Implement: hyperlink registry (OSC 8) + click mapping","description":"Goal
- Track OSC 8 hyperlinks in a compact registry and expose hit-testing hooks for the web renderer.

Acceptance Criteria
- Fixtures cover overlapping hyperlinks, resets, and clear-on-scroll behavior.
- API supports mapping cell->url efficiently.

-{"id":"bd-lff4p.1.8","title":"Implement: incremental patch API for renderer (dirty spans, diff runs)","description":"Goal
- Provide an efficient change representation so renderers can update incrementally.

Requirements
- Dirty tracking at row + span granularity.
- Stable, deterministic patch ordering.
- APIs designed for both WebGPU and native present paths.

Acceptance Criteria
- Benchmarks demonstrate sub-linear updates for typical workloads.

- Stable, deterministic patch ordering.
- APIs designed for both WebGPU and native present paths.

Acceptance Criteria

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.1.9","title":"Test: proptest invariants for grid + parser (never panic; stable semantics)","description":"Goal
- Add property tests that make it safe to aggressively optimize and refactor.

Invariants (examples)
- Cursor always within bounds.
- Lines never exceed width in cell count.
- Parser accepts arbitrary bytes without panic.
- Applying a patch preserves semantic equality vs full recompute.

Acceptance Criteria
- Tests run in CI with fixed seeds + shrinkable counterexamples.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:13.480485759Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:13.480485759Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.9","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:13.480485759Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.9","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:13.480485759Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.9","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:13.480485759Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.9","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:13.480485759Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.10","title":"FrankenTerm Remote: bidirectional protocol + PTY/websocket bridge","description":"Why
- Replacing xterm.js means we must support the full bidirectional terminal contract:
  - output parsing (VT/ANSI)
  - input encoding (keyboard/mouse -> bytes)
  - terminal replies to application queries (DSR/DA/DEC)
  - transport to a real PTY (local or remote)

Scope
- A minimal but correct PTY bridge (Rust) that can run locally and in production contexts.
- A secure websocket protocol for browser <-> PTY.
- End-to-end demos: connect FrankenTerm.WASM to a real shell and run scripted sessions.

Non-Negotiables
- Deterministic trace logging (JSONL) for every E2E session.
- Security: origin restrictions, explicit allowlists, no arbitrary command execution in prod.

Success Criteria
- Browser terminal can connect to a PTY bridge, run commands, and render accurately.
- Golden transcript tests prevent regressions.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:11:38.021410960Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T09:11:38.021410960Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.10","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:11:38.021410960Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.10.1","title":"Spec: FrankenTerm websocket protocol + threat model","description":"Goal
- Define the browser <-> PTY transport protocol and a production-safe threat model.

Must Cover
- Message types
  - input bytes / semantic events
  - resize
  - output chunks
  - terminal replies (DSR/DA/DEC)
  - clipboard (optional)
  - keepalive
- Versioning + schema
  - protocol version negotiation
  - JSON Schema / binary schema (explicit)
- Backpressure + flow control (prevent OOM)
  - bounded queues
  - coalescing policies (resize storms, mouse move floods)
  - fairness: interactive input must not be starved by output
- Security model
  - origin restrictions, auth, rate limits
  - allowed commands (if any), sandboxing
  - no implicit file access
  - explicit threat model + mitigations
- Capability identity
  - TERM/capability profile story for remote PTY sessions (ties to terminfo bead)
- Logging
  - every session emits JSONL with run_id/session_id, git_sha/build_id, sizes, wire counters, latency histograms

Acceptance Criteria
- Protocol is versioned and has a schema.
- E2E scripts can generate deterministic transcripts for golden checks.
- Threat model includes an explicit loss matrix (false allow vs false block) and a documented decision policy.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:06.729410386Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T09:24:57.868100267Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.10.1","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:06.729410386Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
@@ -1355,7 +1355,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.2.4","title":"Implement: glyph rasterization + atlas cache (monospace first)","description":"Goal
- Turn glyphs into atlas textures with stable metrics and minimal overhead.

Requirements
- Start with monospace ASCII + box drawing + common symbols; expand to full Unicode coverage via bd-lff4p.2.18.
- Deterministic glyph keys/ids across runs and platforms (within a given font build).
- Cache with explicit eviction policy + metrics (hit/miss, bytes, upload cost).
- Hot path: no allocations when glyph already cached.

Testing
- Unit tests for key stability and eviction invariants.
- Golden fixtures for a representative glyph set (metrics + atlas coordinates).
- Bench harness integration to measure cache miss cost and steady-state throughput.

Acceptance Criteria
- Benchable API: given a glyph key, returns atlas coords and advances with no allocations in hot path.
- Upload + packing strategy is measurable and regression-gated.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:59.289665299Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T09:19:51.394478179Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.4","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:59.289665299Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.4","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:59.289665299Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.4","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:59.289665299Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.4","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:59.289665299Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.2.5","title":"Implement: WebGPU text rendering (atlas + instanced quads)","description":"Goal
- Render terminal text cells using the glyph atlas and an instanced quad pipeline.

Requirements
- Respect per-cell fg color + attrs (bold/italic/underline as feasible).
- Handle wide glyphs and combining marks (at least gracefully).

Acceptance Criteria
- 120x40 grid @ 60fps with typical demo workloads on modern desktop.
- Instrumented frame timing (CPU submit + GPU time if possible).
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:06.502429115Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:06.502429115Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.5","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:06.502429115Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.5","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:06.502429115Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.5","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:06.502429115Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.5","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:06.502429115Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.2.6","title":"Implement: cursor + selection + hyperlink hover/click rendering","description":"Goal
- Make the terminal feel interactive: cursor, selection visualization, and hyperlink affordances.

Requirements
- Cursor rendering styles: block, bar, underline.
- Selection rendering + copy.
- Hyperlink hover underline + click dispatch.

Acceptance Criteria
- Deterministic visual tests (golden images or checksumable framebuffer dumps).
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:12.226346176Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:12.226346176Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.6","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:12.226346176Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.6","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:12.226346176Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.6","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:12.226346176Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.6","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:12.226346176Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-lff4p.2.7","title":"Implement: web input capture layer (keyboard/mouse/touch)","description":"Goal
- Capture browser input events and feed them into the ftui/FrankenTerm world with minimal latency and deterministic semantics.

Requirements
- Keyboard
  - consistent modifier semantics across browsers/OS
  - repeat handling
  - special keys and function keys
  - composition events integrate with IME plan (bd-lff4p.2.9)
- Mouse
  - click/drag/wheel with correct cell coordinates (respects DPR + font metrics)
  - selection gestures
- Touch
  - tap, long-press, scroll; optional pinch zoom

Testing + Logging
- Unit tests for key mapping tables and edge cases (Ctrl/Alt/Meta/Super).
- Property tests for modifier state machines (no stuck modifiers; idempotent focus loss).
- E2E scripts emit JSONL: event counts, encoded bytes (when relevant), and any mapping fallbacks.

Acceptance Criteria
- Deterministic event encoding suitable for record/replay.
- No key ghosting; no stuck modifiers; focus changes handled correctly.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:19.592183195Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T09:20:04.705424348Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.7","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:19.592183195Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.7","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:19.592183195Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.7","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:19.592183195Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.2.8","title":"Implement: clipboard integration (copy/paste) for frankenterm-web","description":"Goal
- Implement copy and paste behaviors expected from a modern terminal.

Acceptance Criteria
- Copy uses selection model; paste injects as input stream.
- Works across Chrome/Safari/Firefox within their security constraints.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:24.704918895Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:24.704918895Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.8","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:24.704918895Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.8","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:24.704918895Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.8","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:24.704918895Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.2.9","title":"Implement: IME/composition support (CJK input)","description":"Goal
- Provide high-quality text input beyond raw key events.

Acceptance Criteria
- Composition events do not corrupt terminal state.
- Works with common IMEs (macOS, Windows, Linux).
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:29.229709842Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:29.229709842Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.9","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:29.229709842Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.9","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:29.229709842Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.3","title":"ftui-web: compile ftui to WASM and run inside FrankenTerm","description":"Scope
- Compile ftui (core/render/layout/style/text/widgets/runtime + demo) to wasm and run inside FrankenTerm surface.
- Replace terminal polling with a step-based, JS-driven event loop.

Deliverables
- New `ftui-web` crate exposing a `WasmApp` API: new, input, resize, tick, render.
- Minimal web harness that runs ftui-demo-showcase fully client-side.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:49:24.446759480Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T08:49:24.446759480Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.3","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:49:24.446759480Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
@@ -1371,9 +1371,9 @@
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.3.9","title":"Optimize: wasm binary size + startup time (LTO, feature gating)","description":"Goal
- Make the web demo load fast and keep wasm artifacts small.

Acceptance Criteria
- Measured size targets with before/after numbers.
- Startup (time-to-first-frame) improved with profiling evidence.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.4","title":"ftui-native backend: replace Crossterm (Unix-first)","description":"Scope
- Replace Crossterm with a first-party backend: raw mode, alt-screen, mouse, bracketed paste, focus events, kitty keyboard, resize, and robust cleanup.
- Drive input via ftui-core InputParser on raw bytes (no Crossterm event translation).

Deliverables
- New backend crate (name TBD) and integration into ftui-core/ftui-runtime.
- PTY + panic-cleanup tests; perf improvements via fewer syscalls + tighter buffering.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.4.1","title":"Implement: new native terminal backend crate (ftui-tty)","description":"Goal
- Add a first-party native terminal backend crate to replace Crossterm.

Scope
- Unix-first (Linux/macOS). Windows later.

Acceptance Criteria
- Minimal API compiles and can be used by ftui-core/ftui-runtime via a backend trait.
- `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]` in the crate.

-{"id":"bd-lff4p.4.2","title":"Implement: Unix raw mode + terminal feature toggles (alt-screen, mouse, paste, focus, kitty)","description":"Goal
- Replace Crossterm terminal mode management with our own, with stronger semantics and fewer surprises.

Requirements
- Raw mode enter/exit.
- Alt screen enter/leave.
- Mouse capture enable/disable.
- Bracketed paste + focus events.
- Kitty keyboard protocol enable/disable.
- Robust Drop-based cleanup + panic safety.

Acceptance Criteria
- PTY tests prove cleanup on normal exit and panic.
- No leaked terminal state (cursor visible, raw mode off, alt-screen restored).

- Replace Crossterm event polling with a tight byte-reader + ftui-core parsing.

Requirements
- Non-blocking or poll-based read with timeout.
- Convert bytes to ftui-core Event via existing InputParser.
- Support resize, focus, bracketed paste, mouse sequences.

Acceptance Criteria
- Input parity tests vs current behavior for core keys and mouse sequences.
- No busy-looping when idle.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.4.5","title":"Integrate: swap ftui runtime to new backend (native path)","description":"Goal
- Wire the new native backend into `ftui-runtime` and remove all direct Crossterm coupling from the runtime hot path.

Non-Negotiable Compatibility
- Behavior must match current demo semantics (input, resize, mouse, paste, focus, kitty keyboard, alt-screen/inline rules).
- One-writer rule and terminal cleanup semantics must remain correct under normal exit and panic/unwind.

Testing + Logging
- Run the existing PTY E2E suite (`tests/e2e/scripts/run_all.sh` or curated subsets) against the new backend.
- E2E logs must validate against `tests/e2e/lib/e2e_jsonl_schema.json` and include backend identity + capability profile.
- Add/extend tests as needed to cover any behavior differences discovered during migration.

Acceptance Criteria
- `ftui-demo-showcase` runs on the new backend with identical user-visible behavior.
- E2E suite is green and produces detailed JSONL logs usable for regression triage.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:54:38.750235369Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T09:14:43.883488942Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:54:38.750235369Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:54:38.750235369Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:54:38.750235369Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:54:38.750235369Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:54:38.750235369Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.4.6","title":"Remove: Crossterm dependency from ftui-core and ftui-runtime","description":"Goal
- Delete Crossterm from the core build graph and own the terminal backend end-to-end.

Acceptance Criteria
- `rg crossterm` shows no production dependencies (tests may still reference during migration, but should be deleted once validated).
- Workspace builds and tests pass.

- Tests validate cleanup on panic/unwind paths.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.5.6","title":"Alien Artifact: formal cost models for caches + scheduling (renderer + parser)","description":"Goal
- Use principled math to design cache eviction, batching, and scheduling with explicit objective functions.

Examples
- Explicit loss functions for cache misses vs memory.
- Queueing/scheduling model for input vs render vs present.
- Evidence ledger: why a policy was chosen, with measured priors/posteriors.

Acceptance Criteria
- Documented models + experiments that justify the final policies.
- Results integrated into code comments and perf docs.

- Validate FrankenTerm correctness via differential testing.

- Feed identical output byte streams to multiple reference emulators.

- Differential mismatches become tracked fixtures with root-cause notes.

-{"id":"bd-lff4p.5.8","title":"QA: extend shared JSONL schema/logging for web + remote terminal sessions","description":"Goal
- Reuse and extend the existing E2E logging infrastructure (`tests/e2e/lib/logging.sh`, schema/validator) so FrankenTerm web + remote sessions produce first-class, debuggable artifacts.

Scope
- Extend `tests/e2e/lib/e2e_jsonl_schema.json` for:
  - browser identity, DPR, GPU adapter info (when available)
  - patch/frame hashes and patch sizes
  - websocket wire counters + latency histograms
- Update validator and examples without breaking existing logs.

Acceptance Criteria
- All new web/remote E2E scripts validate cleanly.
- Logs are detailed enough to diagnose a mismatch without reproducing locally.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.5.9","title":"QA: golden frame/pixel checksum pipeline for web renderer","description":"Goal
- Add a renderer-level golden system so we can prove visual correctness across refactors and optimizations.

Scope
- Define what constitutes a stable golden (framebuffer hash, patch hash, or both).
- Provide fixtures that render deterministic scenes and compute hashes.

Acceptance Criteria
- A mismatch reports: frame index, region summary, and reproduction trace id.
- Gates integrate with CI (time-bounded).

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.6","title":"Spec: FrankenTerm + ftui-web end-to-end North Star architecture","description":"Goal
- Write the definitive, no-compromises system design for: FrankenTerm (terminal engine + renderer) + ftui-web runtime + native backend replacement.

Must Cover
- Layering boundaries (core vs web vs native).
- Data flows: input -> parse -> model update -> render -> present.
- Determinism strategy (record/replay, seeded demos, time sources).
- Safety constraints: safe Rust, panic discipline, no leaked terminal state.
- Performance budgets (p95/p99) and how we will measure them.

Acceptance Criteria
- Concrete module/crate map with responsibility boundaries.
- Explicit invariants + failure modes + rollback strategy.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.7","title":"Process: Extreme Optimization loop for FrankenTerm and ftui-web","description":"Goal
- Make the Extreme Software Optimization loop mandatory across FrankenTerm + ftui-web (core engine, web renderer, native IO).

Mandatory Loop (every perf change)
1. BASELINE: capture p50/p95/p99 + memory; record run_id + git sha
2. PROFILE: CPU + allocations + (web) GPU timing when available
3. OPPORTUNITY MATRIX: Score = Impact x Confidence / Effort; implement only Score >= 2.0
4. IMPLEMENT: exactly one lever per commit; no behavior changes mixed in
5. PROVE: golden outputs + explicit isomorphism proof (ordering/ties/FP/RNG)
6. VERIFY: trace replay + sha256/checksum gates
7. REPEAT: re-profile (hotspots shift)

Deliverables
- Repeatable harnesses
  - Core parser/grid: `cargo bench -p frankenterm-core` + realistic byte-stream corpus
  - Web renderer: in-browser harness that emits machine-readable JSON (frame_time histogram, time_to_first_frame, steady-state memory)
  - Native backend: PTY harness + present-time metrics + syscall profile (e.g. `strace -c`)
- Golden artifact strategy
  - JSONL traces (reuse/extend `tests/e2e/lib/e2e_jsonl_schema.json`)
  - Frame/patch checksums stored in an append-only registry
- Templates
  - Opportunity matrix table + scoring rubric
  - Isomorphism proof template for every perf-related change
  - Regression threshold policy (when CI should fail)

Acceptance Criteria
- For each subsystem (core/web/native) we can run: baseline -> profile -> replay -> gate.
- Every optimization PR includes before/after numbers + a single-lever diff + isomorphism proof + golden verification.
